divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Without this act, chakra does not exist for humanity. The entire ninja civilization is nullified.
if_altered_consequences: Humanity never develops chakra or ninjutsu.
Hagoromo and Hamura are not born, the Six Paths do not exist.
Indra and Asura do not exist, the reincarnation cycle of Madara/Hashirama and Naruto/Sasuke is impossible.
Akatsuki, hidden villages, and the Fourth War have no reason to exist.

divergence_points.name_en: Birth of Indra and Asura, sons of the Sage of the Six Paths
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: The conflict between Indra and Asura generates the reincarnation cycle that structures the entire saga. Without them, no Madara or Naruto.
if_altered_consequences: The ideological conflict of force/love is not transmitted to subsequent generations.
Madara Uchiha and Hashirama Senju are not reincarnations, their destiny is radically different.
Sasuke and Naruto do not carry the heritage of the Six Paths, the Fourth War ends differently.
The resurrection of Kaguya via Black Zetsu becomes improbable without Hagoromo's reversed plan.

divergence_points.name_en: Meeting of young Hashirama and Madara
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: This friendship is the foundation of Konohagakure and all ninja politics. Without it, hidden villages may never emerge.
if_altered_consequences: Konohagakure is never founded, the system of hidden villages does not emerge.
Madara remains an Uchiha warlord without a Senju rival, the Sengoku period extends indefinitely.
The Kyuubi is never restrained by Mokuton, Mito Uzumaki does not become the first jinchuuriki.
The entire Hokage lineage (Tobirama, Hiruzen, Minato, Tsunade, Kakashi, Naruto) does not exist.

divergence_points.name_en: Battle of the Valley of the End
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Madara's presumed death allows Zetsu to recover him and launch the Tsuki no Me plan. Without this outcome, no Tobi.
if_altered_consequences: If Madara truly dies, Zetsu cannot recover him and the Tsuki no Me plan is never transmitted.
If Madara wins, Konohagakure is destroyed and the Hokage lineage is interrupted.
Mito Uzumaki does not become the Kyuubi jinchuuriki, neither does Kushina.
Obito has no mentor and does not become Tobi, eliminating the cause of the Kyuubi attack.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Yahiko's death transforms Nagato into Pain and causes Akatsuki to shift from a pacifist movement to a criminal organization.
if_altered_consequences: Akatsuki remains a peace movement, never hunts the jinchuuriki.
Nagato never activates the Rinnegan in destructive mode, Konohagakure is not attacked.
Jiraiya does not die infiltrating Amegakure.
The plan to collect the bijuu and Kabuto's edo Tensei have no primary vehicle.

divergence_points.name_en: Apparent death of Obito at Kannabi Bridge
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Obito's secret recovery by Madara is the absolute pivot of the Tsuki no Me plan. Obito becomes the engine of nearly all major events.
if_altered_consequences: If Obito survives normally, he does not fall under Madara's influence and remains a Konohagakure ninja.
The Kyuubi attack on Konohagakure does not occur, Minato and Kushina survive.
The Fourth Great Ninja War is never declared, lacking a masked leader.
Kakashi and Naruto grow up with their parents, completely overturning their personalities.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Rin's death at Kakashi's hand definitively breaks Obito and convinces him the world does not deserve to exist.
if_altered_consequences: Obito does not sink into madness, the Tsuki no Me plan loses its primary executor.
The Kyuubi attack on Konohagakure has no trigger, Minato and Kushina survive.
Kakashi is not consumed by guilt, his personal development is radically different.
Nagato never receives the final impulse for the destruction of Konohagakure.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: The death of Minato and Kushina and the sealing of the Kyuubi in Naruto determine his entire existence and the central narrative arc of the series.
if_altered_consequences: Minato and Kushina survive, Naruto grows up with his parents and is not the lonely outcast child.
The Kyuubi is not sealed in Naruto, he does not have access to Kurama's chakra.
Naruto's jinchuuriki mode and Kyuubi Mode do not exist, the Fourth War has a different outcome.
The Uchiha are not blamed, the clan massacre does not occur or is delayed.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: The massacre is Sasuke's foundational trauma and the engine of his entire trajectory. Without it, no vengeance, no defection.
if_altered_consequences: Sasuke does not develop an obsession with vengeance, his Sharingan and Mangekyou Sharingan evolve differently.
Itachi does not join Akatsuki as a spy, the organization loses a crucial source of intelligence.
Sasuke's defection to Orochimaru does not occur, Team 7 remains intact.
Sasuke never kills Danzo, who retains his influence and potentially takes the title of Hokage.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Danzo's theft of Kotoamatsukami deprives the Uchiha of their only peaceful recourse and accelerates Itachi's decision for the massacre.
if_altered_consequences: Kotoamatsukami could have been used on the elders to prevent the coup without massacre.
Danzo does not possess Shisui's Sharingan, his arm is less powerful.
Itachi, deprived of Shisui's trust, could have sought another solution.
Danzo's arc during the Five Kage Summit is profoundly modified.

divergence_points.name_en: Orochimaru marks Sasuke with the Cursed Seal
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: The Cursed Seal is the primary tool that pushes Sasuke toward forbidden power and precipitates his defection to Orochimaru.
if_altered_consequences: Without the seal, Sasuke does not feel the call of Orochimaru's power and defection is far less probable.
Team 7 remains grouped, the trio of Naruto-Sasuke-Sakura faces Orochimaru's invasion together.
Orochimaru loses his long-term plan to obtain Sasuke's body.
The dynamics of the series post-Chunin Exam are entirely restructured.

divergence_points.name_en: Invasion of Konohagakure by Oto and Suna
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Hiruzen's death and Konohagakure's disorganization open the way for Tsunade's rise and durably modify the village's politics.
if_altered_consequences: If Hiruzen survives, he remains Hokage and Tsunade is not recruited, changing the management of the Akatsuki crisis.
If the invasion fails completely, Orochimaru loses his organization and Sasuke has no refuge for his defection.
Hiruzen's death deprives Konohagakure of its wisest advisor at the moment of Akatsuki's rise.
Danzo gains political influence without Hiruzen as a counterweight.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: The defection redefines the arcs of Naruto, Sakura, and Sasuke for the entire Shippuden portion and motivates Naruto's intensive training.
if_altered_consequences: Naruto does not depart for 2.5 years of training with Jiraiya, his progression is different.
Sasuke does not absorb Orochimaru and does not form Team Taka.
Itachi potentially survives, lacking a trained and strong enough Sasuke to defeat him.
The Five Kage Summit does not unfold the same way, Danzo is not killed.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Jiraiya's death deprives Naruto of his mentor and triggers the quest for peace that culminates in reconciliation with Nagato.
if_altered_consequences: Jiraiya surviving could decipher the code alone and face Pain with Naruto, changing the battle's outcome.
Naruto is not driven by grief to enter berserk bijuu mode during the Konohagakure attack.
Naruto's conversation with Nagato would have different dynamics with Jiraiya present.
Naruto loses an essential guide for navigating the Fourth Great Ninja War.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Pain's attack and the resulting reconciliation transform Nagato from enemy to posthumous ally and give Naruto his vision of peace.
if_altered_consequences: If Nagato is not convinced, he does not resurrect the victims and Akatsuki remains a direct threat.
If Naruto fails, the Kyuubi is extracted and he dies, the war is lost from the start.
The massive destruction of Konohagakure delays mobilization for the Fourth War.
Jiraiya's pacifist message transmitted through Naruto never reaches Nagato.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Itachi's death and the revelation of truth by Tobi are the pivot that orients Sasuke against Konohagakure rather than toward it.
if_altered_consequences: If Itachi reveals the truth himself before dying, Sasuke is not manipulated by Tobi.
Sasuke does not join Akatsuki and does not attempt to attack the Five Kage Summit.
Danzo potentially survives, retaining his power and his Sharingan.
Sasuke could join the alliance rather than fight it during the Fourth War.

divergence_points.name_en: Beginning of the Fourth Great Ninja War
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: The war forces all narrative threads to converge. It is the crucible where all revelations and reconciliations are accomplished.
if_altered_consequences: Without the war, Madara is never resurrected and the Tsuki no Me plan fails in the shadows.
Naruto and Sasuke never receive the chakra of the Six Paths from Hagoromo.
Obito's redemption and Kaguya's resurrection have no framework to occur.
The alliance between the five villages is not consolidated, post-war tensions persist.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Obito's redemption and the transfer of the Rinne Sharingan allow Kakashi to obtain both Mangekyou and contribute to Kaguya's defeat.
if_altered_consequences: Without redemption, Obito remains hostile and Madara's resurrection is not countered from within.
Kakashi does not temporarily receive both Mangekyou, he cannot use the perfect Susanoo against Kaguya.
The causal chain leading to final victory over Kaguya is broken.
The moral legacy of Minato and Rin remains tainted, affecting the series' thematic conclusion.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Kaguya's defeat is the resolution of the millennial threat and conditions the survival of all ninja civilization.
if_altered_consequences: If Kaguya is not sealed, the Mugen Tsukuyomi absorbs all humanity into eternal illusion.
Naruto and Sasuke do not receive the mark of the Six Paths, their final battle is impossible.
The period of peace that founds the Boruto era cannot begin.
All post-war existence, including the birth of Boruto and Sarada, is nullified.

divergence_points.name_en: Final battle of Naruto against Sasuke
divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: This battle decides whether Sasuke establishes a dictatorship or the world enters an era of peace. Reconciliation founds the entire Boruto era.
if_altered_consequences: If Sasuke wins, he destroys the old system by force and establishes an authoritarian regime.
If Naruto dies, the Konohagakure jinchuuriki lineage ends and the Seventh Hokage does not exist.
Without reconciliation, Boruto is born into a world without lasting peace or a Sasuke father figure.
The Kara project and subsequent Otsutsuki threat are faced without the Naruto-Sasuke alliance.

divergence_points.why_pivotal_en: Kurama's death deprives Naruto of his primary power source and weakens him against subsequent Otsutsuki threats.
if_altered_consequences: Naruto retains his basic Kyuubi powers and remains a major deterrent force in the Boruto era.
The power balance against Isshiki is different, Baryon Mode is not necessary.
Naruto's vulnerability in late Boruto arcs is reduced, changing the narrative dynamics.
Kurama's death removes a central character who had himself completed a full redemption arc.
